diagrams.title: Default Argument Assignment Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how default values are assigned when function is called with fewer arguments than declared
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how compiler fills in missing arguments from right to left

diagrams.title: Valid vs Invalid Default Argument Placement
diagrams.description: Demonstrates correct and incorrect ways to specify default arguments in function parameters
elements: Valid: void f(int a, int b=10, int c=20)
Invalid: void f(int a=5, int b, int c=10)
Invalid: void f(int a=1, int b=2, int c, int d)
diagrams.purpose: Memorizing the right-to-left placement rule for default arguments

diagrams.title: Declaration vs Definition for Defaults
diagrams.description: Shows where default arguments should and should not be specified
elements: Both places with defaults: ✗ Compilation Error
diagrams.purpose: Remembering that defaults only go in declaration, not definition
